Originally addressed to systemically important banks (SIBs), these 14 principles cover governance, data architecture, data quality, reporting practices, and supervisory review. Widely adopted as a global benchmark for data risk management in banking.
clause_text: A bank should be able to capture and aggregate all material risk data across the banking group. Data should be available by business line, legal entity, asset type, industry, region and other groupings, as relevant for the risk in question. A bank's risk data aggregation capabilities should include all material risk exposures, including those that are off-balance sheet.
mapping_rationale: Core data completeness requirement across all dimensions and entities

regulation_name: Principles for Effective Risk Data Aggregation and Risk Reporting
regulation_description: Principles issued by the Basel Committee on Banking Supervision (BCBS) establishing expectations for banks' risk data aggregation capabilities and risk reporting practices. Originally addressed to systemically important banks (SIBs), these 14 principles cover governance, data architecture, data quality, reporting practices, and supervisory review. Widely adopted as a global benchmark for data risk management in banking.
clause_text: A banking organisation is not required to express all forms of risk in a common metric or basis, but risk data aggregation capabilities should be the same regardless of the choice of risk aggregation systems implemented. Each system should make clear the specific approach used to aggregate exposures for any given risk measure, in order to allow the board and senior management to assess the results properly.
mapping_rationale: Data consistency in aggregation methodology; Accountability for transparent methodology communication

regulation_name: Principles for Effective Risk Data Aggregation and Risk Reporting
regulation_description: Principles issued by the Basel Committee on Banking Supervision (BCBS) establishing expectations for banks' risk data aggregation capabilities and risk reporting practices. Originally addressed to systemically important banks (SIBs), these 14 principles cover governance, data architecture, data quality, reporting practices, and supervisory review. Widely adopted as a global benchmark for data risk management in banking.
clause_text: Banks should produce aggregated risk data that is complete and should measure and monitor the completeness of their risk data. Where risk data is not entirely complete, the impact should not be critical to the bank's ability to manage its risks effectively. Banks' data should be materially complete, with any exceptions identified and explained.
mapping_rationale: Data completeness monitoring and exception management requirement

regulation_name: Principles for Effective Risk Data Aggregation and Risk Reporting
regulation_description: Principles issued by the Basel Committee on Banking Supervision (BCBS) establishing expectations for banks' risk data aggregation capabilities and risk reporting practices. Originally addressed to systemically important banks (SIBs), these 14 principles cover governance, data architecture, data quality, reporting practices, and supervisory review. Widely adopted as a global benchmark for data risk management in banking.
clause_text: A bank should be able to generate aggregate and up-to-date risk data in a timely manner while also meeting the principles relating to accuracy and integrity, completeness and adaptability. A bank's risk data aggregation capabilities should ensure that it is able to produce aggregate risk information on a timely basis to meet all risk management reporting requirements.
mapping_rationale: Core data timeliness requirement for risk reporting

regulation_name: Principles for Effective Risk Data Aggregation and Risk Reporting
regulation_description: Principles issued by the Basel Committee on Banking Supervision (BCBS) establishing expectations for banks' risk data aggregation capabilities and risk reporting practices. Originally addressed to systemically important banks (SIBs), these 14 principles cover governance, data architecture, data quality, reporting practices, and supervisory review. Widely adopted as a global benchmark for data risk management in banking.
clause_text: Different types of data will be required at different speeds, depending on the type of risk. Banks need to build their risk systems to be capable of producing aggregated risk data rapidly during times of stress/crisis for all critical risks. Critical risks include: aggregated credit exposure to large corporate borrowers, counterparty credit risk exposures including derivatives, trading exposures and market concentrations, liquidity risk indicators such as cash flows and funding, and operational risk indicators that are time-critical.
mapping_rationale: Timeliness during stress for critical risk types; Regulatory compliance for crisis data capability

regulation_name: Principles for Effective Risk Data Aggregation and Risk Reporting
regulation_description: Principles issued by the Basel Committee on Banking Supervision (BCBS) establishing expectations for banks' risk data aggregation capabilities and risk reporting practices. Originally addressed to systemically important banks (SIBs), these 14 principles cover governance, data architecture, data quality, reporting practices, and supervisory review. Widely adopted as a global benchmark for data risk management in banking.
clause_text: Supervisors will review that the bank specific frequency requirements, for both normal and stress/crisis situations, generate aggregate and up-to-date risk data in a timely manner.
mapping_rationale: Defined frequency requirements for normal and stress conditions; Regulatory compliance readiness

regulation_name: Principles for Effective Risk Data Aggregation and Risk Reporting
regulation_description: Principles issued by the Basel Committee on Banking Supervision (BCBS) establishing expectations for banks' risk data aggregation capabilities and risk reporting practices. Originally addressed to systemically important banks (SIBs), these 14 principles cover governance, data architecture, data quality, reporting practices, and supervisory review. Widely adopted as a global benchmark for data risk management in banking.
clause_text: A bank should be able to generate aggregate risk data to meet a broad range of on-demand, ad hoc risk management reporting requests, including requests during stress/crisis situations, requests due to changing internal needs and requests to meet supervisory queries. A bank's risk data aggregation capabilities should be flexible and adaptable to meet ad hoc data requests and to assess emerging risks.
mapping_rationale: Timeliness for ad hoc and stress reporting; Regulatory compliance for supervisory query capability

regulation_name: Principles for Effective Risk Data Aggregation and Risk Reporting
regulation_description: Principles issued by the Basel Committee on Banking Supervision (BCBS) establishing expectations for banks' risk data aggregation capabilities and risk reporting practices. Originally addressed to systemically important banks (SIBs), these 14 principles cover governance, data architecture, data quality, reporting practices, and supervisory review. Widely adopted as a global benchmark for data risk management in banking.
clause_text: Adaptability includes: data aggregation processes that are flexible and enable risk data to be aggregated for assessment and quick decision-making; and capabilities for data customisation to users' needs (eg dashboards, key takeaways, anomalies), to drill down as needed, and to produce quick summary reports.
mapping_rationale: Timeliness through flexible aggregation; Completeness through drill-down and customisation

regulation_name: Principles for Effective Risk Data Aggregation and Risk Reporting
regulation_description: Principles issued by the Basel Committee on Banking Supervision (BCBS) establishing expectations for banks' risk data aggregation capabilities and risk reporting practices. Originally addressed to systemically important banks (SIBs), these 14 principles cover governance, data architecture, data quality, reporting practices, and supervisory review. Widely adopted as a global benchmark for data risk management in banking.
clause_text: Adaptability includes: capabilities to incorporate new developments on the organisation of the business and/or external factors that influence the bank's risk profile; and capabilities to incorporate changes in the regulatory framework.
mapping_rationale: Regulatory compliance adaptability for evolving requirements

regulation_name: Principles for Effective Risk Data Aggregation and Risk Reporting
regulation_description: Principles issued by the Basel Committee on Banking Supervision (BCBS) establishing expectations for banks' risk data aggregation capabilities and risk reporting practices. Originally addressed to systemically important banks (SIBs), these 14 principles cover governance, data architecture, data quality, reporting practices, and supervisory review. Widely adopted as a global benchmark for data risk management in banking.
clause_text: Banks should be able to generate subsets of data based on requested scenarios or resulting from economic events. For example, a bank should be able to aggregate risk data quickly on country credit exposures as of a specified date based on a list of countries, as well as industry credit exposures across all business lines and geographic areas.
mapping_rationale: Timeliness for on-demand scenario data; Completeness of exposure data across dimensions

regulation_name: Principles for Effective Risk Data Aggregation and Risk Reporting
regulation_description: Principles issued by the Basel Committee on Banking Supervision (BCBS) establishing expectations for banks' risk data aggregation capabilities and risk reporting practices. Originally addressed to systemically important banks (SIBs), these 14 principles cover governance, data architecture, data quality, reporting practices, and supervisory review. Widely adopted as a global benchmark for data risk management in banking.
clause_text: Risk management reports should accurately and precisely convey aggregated risk data and reflect risk in an exact manner. Reports should be reconciled and validated. A bank's board and senior management should rely with confidence on the aggregated information to make critical decisions about risk.
mapping_rationale: Data accuracy in risk reports; Data validity through reconciliation and validation

regulation_name: Principles for Effective Risk Data Aggregation and Risk Reporting
regulation_description: Principles issued by the Basel Committee on Banking Supervision (BCBS) establishing expectations for banks' risk data aggregation capabilities and risk reporting practices. Originally addressed to systemically important banks (SIBs), these 14 principles cover governance, data architecture, data quality, reporting practices, and supervisory review. Widely adopted as a global benchmark for data risk management in banking.
clause_text: To ensure the accuracy of the reports, a bank should maintain: defined requirements and processes to reconcile reports to risk data; automated and manual edit and reasonableness checks, including an inventory of the validation rules applied to quantitative information; and integrated procedures for identifying, reporting and explaining data errors or weaknesses in data integrity via exceptions reports.
mapping_rationale: Data validity through validation rules and edit checks; Data accuracy through reconciliation processes

regulation_name: Principles for Effective Risk Data Aggregation and Risk Reporting
regulation_description: Principles issued by the Basel Committee on Banking Supervision (BCBS) establishing expectations for banks' risk data aggregation capabilities and risk reporting practices. Originally addressed to systemically important banks (SIBs), these 14 principles cover governance, data architecture, data quality, reporting practices, and supervisory review. Widely adopted as a global benchmark for data risk management in banking.
clause_text: Approximations are an integral part of risk reporting and risk management. Results from models, scenario analyses, and stress testing are examples of approximations that provide critical information for managing risk. Banks should establish expectations for the reliability of approximations (accuracy, timeliness, etc) to ensure that management can rely with confidence on the information to make critical decisions about risk.
mapping_rationale: Data validity standards for model approximations; Regulatory compliance for model output reliability

regulation_name: Principles for Effective Risk Data Aggregation and Risk Reporting
regulation_description: Principles issued by the Basel Committee on Banking Supervision (BCBS) establishing expectations for banks' risk data aggregation capabilities and risk reporting practices. Originally addressed to systemically important banks (SIBs), these 14 principles cover governance, data architecture, data quality, reporting practices, and supervisory review. Widely adopted as a global benchmark for data risk management in banking.
clause_text: Senior management should establish accuracy and precision requirements for both regular and stress/crisis reporting, including critical position and exposure information. Banks should consider accuracy requirements analogous to accounting materiality. If omission or misstatement could influence the risk decisions of users, this may be considered material. A bank should be able to support the rationale for accuracy requirements based on validation, testing or reconciliation processes.
mapping_rationale: Data accuracy standards analogous to accounting; Data validity thresholds; Policy adherence for accuracy requirements

regulation_name: Principles for Effective Risk Data Aggregation and Risk Reporting
regulation_description: Principles issued by the Basel Committee on Banking Supervision (BCBS) establishing expectations for banks' risk data aggregation capabilities and risk reporting practices. Originally addressed to systemically important banks (SIBs), these 14 principles cover governance, data architecture, data quality, reporting practices, and supervisory review. Widely adopted as a global benchmark for data risk management in banking.
clause_text: Risk management reports should cover all material risk areas within the organisation. Reports should include exposure and position information for all significant risk areas (eg credit risk, market risk, liquidity risk, operational risk) and all significant components of those risk areas. Risk management reports should also cover risk-related measures (eg regulatory and economic capital).
mapping_rationale: Data completeness across all material risk areas; Disclosure completeness for risk reporting

regulation_name: Principles for Effective Risk Data Aggregation and Risk Reporting
regulation_description: Principles issued by the Basel Committee on Banking Supervision (BCBS) establishing expectations for banks' risk data aggregation capabilities and risk reporting practices. Originally addressed to systemically important banks (SIBs), these 14 principles cover governance, data architecture, data quality, reporting practices, and supervisory review. Widely adopted as a global benchmark for data risk management in banking.
clause_text: Reports should identify emerging risk concentrations, provide information in the context of limits and risk appetite/tolerance and propose recommendations for action where appropriate. Risk reports should include the current status of measures agreed by the board or senior management to reduce risk. This includes providing the ability to monitor emerging trends through forward-looking forecasts and stress tests.
mapping_rationale: Data completeness for concentration and forward-looking reporting; Disclosure completeness for risk limits

regulation_name: Principles for Effective Risk Data Aggregation and Risk Reporting
regulation_description: Principles issued by the Basel Committee on Banking Supervision (BCBS) establishing expectations for banks' risk data aggregation capabilities and risk reporting practices. Originally addressed to systemically important banks (SIBs), these 14 principles cover governance, data architecture, data quality, reporting practices, and supervisory review. Widely adopted as a global benchmark for data risk management in banking.
clause_text: Banks should determine risk reporting requirements that best suit their own business models and risk profiles. An aggregated risk report should include, but not be limited to: capital adequacy, regulatory capital, capital and liquidity ratio projections, credit risk, market risk, operational risk, liquidity risk, stress testing results, inter- and intra-risk concentrations, and funding positions and plans.
mapping_rationale: Data completeness for minimum aggregated report content; Disclosure completeness requirements

regulation_name: Principles for Effective Risk Data Aggregation and Risk Reporting
regulation_description: Principles issued by the Basel Committee on Banking Supervision (BCBS) establishing expectations for banks' risk data aggregation capabilities and risk reporting practices. Originally addressed to systemically important banks (SIBs), these 14 principles cover governance, data architecture, data quality, reporting practices, and supervisory review. Widely adopted as a global benchmark for data risk management in banking.
clause_text: Risk management reports to the board and senior management should provide a forward-looking assessment of risk and should not just rely on current and past data. The reports should contain forecasts or scenarios for key market variables and the effects on the bank so as to inform the board and senior management of the likely trajectory of the bank's capital and risk profile in the future.
mapping_rationale: Data completeness for forward-looking content; Timeliness of scenario-based reporting

regulation_name: Principles for Effective Risk Data Aggregation and Risk Reporting
regulation_description: Principles issued by the Basel Committee on Banking Supervision (BCBS) establishing expectations for banks' risk data aggregation capabilities and risk reporting practices. Originally addressed to systemically important banks (SIBs), these 14 principles cover governance, data architecture, data quality, reporting practices, and supervisory review. Widely adopted as a global benchmark for data risk management in banking.
clause_text: Risk management reports should communicate information in a clear and concise manner. Reports should be easy to understand yet comprehensive enough to facilitate informed decision-making. Reports should include meaningful information tailored to the needs of the recipients. Reports should include an appropriate balance between risk data, analysis and interpretation, and qualitative explanations. Higher up in the organisation, more aggregation is expected and a greater degree of qualitative interpretation will be necessary.
mapping_rationale: Disclosure accuracy — reports must not be misleading; Disclosure accessibility — tailored to recipients

regulation_name: Principles for Effective Risk Data Aggregation and Risk Reporting
regulation_description: Principles issued by the Basel Committee on Banking Supervision (BCBS) establishing expectations for banks' risk data aggregation capabilities and risk reporting practices. Originally addressed to systemically important banks (SIBs), these 14 principles cover governance, data architecture, data quality, reporting practices, and supervisory review. Widely adopted as a global benchmark for data risk management in banking.
clause_text: Reporting policies and procedures should recognise the differing information needs of the board, senior management, and other levels of the organisation. The bank's board is responsible for determining its own risk reporting requirements. The board should alert senior management when risk reports do not meet its requirements. Senior management is also responsible for determining its own risk reporting requirements and ensuring it is receiving relevant information.
mapping_rationale: Policy adherence for differentiated reporting; Accountability for reporting governance

regulation_name: Principles for Effective Risk Data Aggregation and Risk Reporting
regulation_description: Principles issued by the Basel Committee on Banking Supervision (BCBS) establishing expectations for banks' risk data aggregation capabilities and risk reporting practices. Originally addressed to systemically important banks (SIBs), these 14 principles cover governance, data architecture, data quality, reporting practices, and supervisory review. Widely adopted as a global benchmark for data risk management in banking.
clause_text: A bank should develop an inventory and classification of risk data items which includes a reference to the concepts used to elaborate the reports.
mapping_rationale: Data completeness through inventory; Accountability for data classification and cataloguing

regulation_name: Principles for Effective Risk Data Aggregation and Risk Reporting
regulation_description: Principles issued by the Basel Committee on Banking Supervision (BCBS) establishing expectations for banks' risk data aggregation capabilities and risk reporting practices. Originally addressed to systemically important banks (SIBs), these 14 principles cover governance, data architecture, data quality, reporting practices, and supervisory review. Widely adopted as a global benchmark for data risk management in banking.
clause_text: A bank should confirm periodically with recipients that the information aggregated and reported is relevant and appropriate, in terms of both amount and quality, to the governance and decision-making process.
mapping_rationale: Policy adherence through periodic validation of report relevance with stakeholders

regulation_name: Principles for Effective Risk Data Aggregation and Risk Reporting
regulation_description: Principles issued by the Basel Committee on Banking Supervision (BCBS) establishing expectations for banks' risk data aggregation capabilities and risk reporting practices. Originally addressed to systemically important banks (SIBs), these 14 principles cover governance, data architecture, data quality, reporting practices, and supervisory review. Widely adopted as a global benchmark for data risk management in banking.
clause_text: The board and senior management should set the frequency of risk management report production and distribution. The frequency of risk reports will vary according to the type of risk, purpose and recipients. A bank should assess periodically the purpose of each report and set requirements for how quickly the reports need to be produced in both normal and stress/crisis situations. A bank should routinely test its ability to produce accurate reports within established timeframes, particularly in stress/crisis situations.
mapping_rationale: Data timeliness for defined report frequencies; Disclosure timeliness for stress/crisis capability

regulation_name: Principles for Effective Risk Data Aggregation and Risk Reporting
regulation_description: Principles issued by the Basel Committee on Banking Supervision (BCBS) establishing expectations for banks' risk data aggregation capabilities and risk reporting practices. Originally addressed to systemically important banks (SIBs), these 14 principles cover governance, data architecture, data quality, reporting practices, and supervisory review. Widely adopted as a global benchmark for data risk management in banking.
clause_text: In times of stress/crisis all relevant and critical credit, market and liquidity position/exposure reports should be available within a very short period of time to react effectively to evolving risks. Some position/exposure information may be needed immediately (intraday) to allow for timely and effective reactions.
mapping_rationale: Data timeliness — near-real-time capability for critical risk positions during stress

regulation_name: Principles for Effective Risk Data Aggregation and Risk Reporting
regulation_description: Principles issued by the Basel Committee on Banking Supervision (BCBS) establishing expectations for banks' risk data aggregation capabilities and risk reporting practices. Originally addressed to systemically important banks (SIBs), these 14 principles cover governance, data architecture, data quality, reporting practices, and supervisory review. Widely adopted as a global benchmark for data risk management in banking.
clause_text: Risk management reports should be distributed to the relevant parties while ensuring confidentiality is maintained. Procedures should be in place to allow for rapid collection and analysis of risk data and timely dissemination of reports to all appropriate recipients. This should be balanced with the need to ensure confidentiality as appropriate. A bank should confirm periodically that the relevant recipients receive timely reports.
mapping_rationale: Unauthorized access prevention for report distribution; Data leakage prevention; Timeliness of dissemination

regulation_name: Principles for Effective Risk Data Aggregation and Risk Reporting
regulation_description: Principles issued by the Basel Committee on Banking Supervision (BCBS) establishing expectations for banks' risk data aggregation capabilities and risk reporting practices. Originally addressed to systemically important banks (SIBs), these 14 principles cover governance, data architecture, data quality, reporting practices, and supervisory review. Widely adopted as a global benchmark for data risk management in banking.
clause_text: Supervisors should periodically review and evaluate a bank's compliance with the Principles. Supervisors may test a bank's compliance through occasional requests for information on selected risk issues within short deadlines, thereby testing the capacity of a bank to aggregate risk data rapidly and produce risk reports. Supervisors should test a bank's capabilities in both stress/crisis and steady-state environments.
mapping_rationale: Regulatory compliance readiness for supervisory testing; Timeliness for ad hoc supervisory requests

regulation_name: Principles for Effective Risk Data Aggregation and Risk Reporting
regulation_description: Principles issued by the Basel Committee on Banking Supervision (BCBS) establishing expectations for banks' risk data aggregation capabilities and risk reporting practices. Originally addressed to systemically important banks (SIBs), these 14 principles cover governance, data architecture, data quality, reporting practices, and supervisory review. Widely adopted as a global benchmark for data risk management in banking.
clause_text: Supervisors should have and use appropriate tools and resources to require effective and timely remedial action by a bank to address deficiencies in its risk data aggregation capabilities and risk reporting practices. Tools may include requiring remedial action, increasing supervision intensity, requiring independent third-party review, and possible use of capital add-ons under Pillar 2. Supervisors should be able to set limits on a bank's risks or growth in activities where deficiencies in risk data aggregation and reporting are assessed as causing significant weaknesses.
mapping_rationale: Primarily directed at supervisory powers; Bank obligation is implied but clause is supervisor-facing

regulation_name: Principles for Effective Risk Data Aggregation and Risk Reporting
regulation_description: Principles issued by the Basel Committee on Banking Supervision (BCBS) establishing expectations for banks' risk data aggregation capabilities and risk reporting practices. Originally addressed to systemically important banks (SIBs), these 14 principles cover governance, data architecture, data quality, reporting practices, and supervisory review. Widely adopted as a global benchmark for data risk management in banking.
clause_text: Supervisors should cooperate with relevant supervisors in other jurisdictions regarding the supervision and review of the Principles, and the implementation of any remedial action if necessary. Effective cooperation and appropriate information sharing between home and host supervisory authorities should contribute to the robustness of a bank's risk management practices across operations in multiple jurisdictions.
mapping_rationale: Entirely supervisor-directed; No direct bank obligation for data management

regulation_name: Principles for Effective Risk Data Aggregation and Risk Reporting
regulation_description: Principles issued by the Basel Committee on Banking Supervision (BCBS) establishing expectations for banks' risk data aggregation capabilities and risk reporting practices. Originally addressed to systemically important banks (SIBs), these 14 principles cover governance, data architecture, data quality, reporting practices, and supervisory review. Widely adopted as a global benchmark for data risk management in banking.
clause_text: Risk data aggregation means defining, gathering and processing risk data according to the bank's risk reporting requirements to enable the bank to measure its performance against its risk tolerance/appetite. This includes sorting, merging or breaking down sets of data.
mapping_rationale: Foundational definition establishing scope of data aggregation obligations

regulation_name: Principles for Effective Risk Data Aggregation and Risk Reporting
regulation_description: Principles issued by the Basel Committee on Banking Supervision (BCBS) establishing expectations for banks' risk data aggregation capabilities and risk reporting practices. Originally addressed to systemically important banks (SIBs), these 14 principles cover governance, data architecture, data quality, reporting practices, and supervisory review. Widely adopted as a global benchmark for data risk management in banking.
clause_text: These Principles are initially addressed to systemically important banks (SIBs) and apply at both the banking group and on a solo basis. National supervisors may choose to apply the Principles to a wider range of banks proportionate to size, nature and complexity. It is strongly suggested that national supervisors also apply these Principles to banks identified as D-SIBs.
mapping_rationale: Establishes applicability scope; Relevant for D-SIB.

regulation_name: Principles for Effective Risk Data Aggregation and Risk Reporting
regulation_description: Principles issued by the Basel Committee on Banking Supervision (BCBS) establishing expectations for banks' risk data aggregation capabilities and risk reporting practices. Originally addressed to systemically important banks (SIBs), these 14 principles cover governance, data architecture, data quality, reporting practices, and supervisory review. Widely adopted as a global benchmark for data risk management in banking.
clause_text: The Principles apply to a bank's risk management data, including data that is critical to enabling the bank to manage the risks it faces. Risk data and reports should provide management with the ability to monitor and track risks relative to the bank's risk tolerance/appetite. These Principles also apply to all key internal risk management models, including but not limited to Pillar 1 regulatory capital models and Pillar 2 capital models.
mapping_rationale: Regulatory compliance scope covering all risk data and models; Data accuracy for model inputs

regulation_name: Principles for Effective Risk Data Aggregation and Risk Reporting
regulation_description: Principles issued by the Basel Committee on Banking Supervision (BCBS) establishing expectations for banks' risk data aggregation capabilities and risk reporting practices. Originally addressed to systemically important banks (SIBs), these 14 principles cover governance, data architecture, data quality, reporting practices, and supervisory review. Widely adopted as a global benchmark for data risk management in banking.
clause_text: All the Principles included in this paper are also applicable to processes that have been outsourced to third parties.
mapping_rationale: Extends all data governance obligations to outsourced/third-party processes

regulation_name: Principles for Effective Risk Data Aggregation and Risk Reporting
regulation_description: Principles issued by the Basel Committee on Banking Supervision (BCBS) establishing expectations for banks' risk data aggregation capabilities and risk reporting practices. Originally addressed to systemically important banks (SIBs), these 14 principles cover governance, data architecture, data quality, reporting practices, and supervisory review. Widely adopted as a global benchmark for data risk management in banking.
clause_text: Banks should meet all risk data aggregation and risk reporting principles simultaneously. However, trade-offs among Principles could be accepted in exceptional circumstances such as urgent/ad hoc requests. There should be no trade-offs that materially impact risk management decisions. Banks should have policies and processes in place regarding the application of trade-offs and should be able to explain the impact through qualitative reports and quantitative measures.
mapping_rationale: Policy adherence for managing data principle trade-offs; Regulatory compliance for exception governance

regulation_name: Principles for Effective Risk Data Aggregation and Risk Reporting
regulation_description: Principles issued by the Basel Committee on Banking Supervision (BCBS) establishing expectations for banks' risk data aggregation capabilities and risk reporting practices. Originally addressed to systemically important banks (SIBs), these 14 principles cover governance, data architecture, data quality, reporting practices, and supervisory review. Widely adopted as a global benchmark for data risk management in banking.
clause_text: The concept of materiality means that data and reports can exceptionally exclude information only if it does not affect the decision-making process in a bank. Banks should take into account the type of risks involved, the evolving nature of banking business, and the potential future impact of excluded information on decision-making.
mapping_rationale: Data completeness materiality threshold for decision-making

regulation_name: Principles for Effective Risk Data Aggregation and Risk Reporting
regulation_description: Principles issued by the Basel Committee on Banking Supervision (BCBS) establishing expectations for banks' risk data aggregation capabilities and risk reporting practices. Originally addressed to systemically important banks (SIBs), these 14 principles cover governance, data architecture, data quality, reporting practices, and supervisory review. Widely adopted as a global benchmark for data risk management in banking.
clause_text: Banks should develop forward looking reporting capabilities to provide early warnings of any potential breaches of risk limits that may exceed the bank's risk tolerance/appetite. These risk reporting capabilities should also allow banks to conduct flexible and effective stress testing which is capable of providing forward-looking risk assessments.
mapping_rationale: Data timeliness for early warning capabilities; Data completeness for forward-looking assessments

regulation_name: Principles for Effective Risk Data Aggregation and Risk Reporting
regulation_description: Principles issued by the Basel Committee on Banking Supervision (BCBS) establishing expectations for banks' risk data aggregation capabilities and risk reporting practices. Originally addressed to systemically important banks (SIBs), these 14 principles cover governance, data architecture, data quality, reporting practices, and supervisory review. Widely adopted as a global benchmark for data risk management in banking.
clause_text: Expert judgment may occasionally be applied to incomplete data to facilitate the aggregation process, as well as the interpretation of results within the risk reporting process. Reliance on expert judgment in place of complete and accurate data should occur only on an exception basis, and should not materially impact compliance. When expert judgment is applied, the process should be clearly documented and transparent so as to allow for an independent review.
mapping_rationale: Data accuracy when expert judgment substitutes for complete data; Records management for documentation
